Defendant Buffalo Pumps, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Transferred From D.302 Per Recusal Of Judge Mahoney)
Off Calendar Apr-19-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Grant Walters, Esq. of Brayton & Purcell, 415-898-1555 for plaintiff and opposing party Calvin Oxford

RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT(TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT.302 PER RECUSAL OF JUDGE MAHONEY) IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN AND THE CRANE DECLARATION IS INSUFFICIENT TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT CONCERNING PLAINTIFF'S EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS FROM DEFENDANT'S PUMP. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO CRANE'S STATEMENT THAT "ALL BOILERMAKERS WORKED ON ALL PUMPS" IS SUSTAINED FOR LACK OF FOUNDATION. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF STATED THAT THE MATTER WAS RESOLVED LATE THE NIGHT BEFORE. THE COURT COULD NOT TAKE THE MOTION OFF CALENDAR WITHOUT THE MOVING PARTY PRESENT. THEREFORE THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. Prevailing party to submit form of order. (301/PJB/AP) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189
